rationale: Both papers are tightly aligned on prescription-drug compliance behavior using observed longitudinal purchase/refill timing and explicitly include advertising effects (including heterogeneity), not just generic background. The cited 2004 JMR paper develops a compliance model in chronic drug categories (including hyperlipidemia), which plausibly serves as a conceptual/method benchmark for the 2005 DTCA-compliance study, so this is not an obvious incidental mention.
